issue.message: Missing permissions required by ActivityRecognitionClient.requestActivityUpdates: android.permission.ACTIVITY_RECOGNITION or com.google.android.gms.permission.ACTIVITY_RECOGNITION
Missing permissions required by ActivityRecognitionClient.removeActivityUpdates: android.permission.ACTIVITY_RECOGNITION or com.google.android.gms.permission.ACTIVITY_RECOGNITION
Missing permissions required by FusedLocationProviderClient.requestLocationUpdates: android.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATION or android.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION
Missing permissions required by FusedLocationProviderClient.requestLocationUpdates: android.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATION or android.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION
Missing permissions required by FusedLocationProviderClient.requestLocationUpdates: android.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATION or android.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION
This field should be annotated with `ChecksSdkIntAtLeast(extension=0)`
Consider using `apply()` instead; `commit` writes its data to persistent storage immediately, whereas `apply` will handle it in the background
issue.errorLine1: .commit() // Use commit() for immediate write-through (not apply())

issue.message: Consider using `apply()` instead; `commit` writes its data to persistent storage immediately, whereas `apply` will handle it in the background
Consider using `apply()` instead; `commit` writes its data to persistent storage immediately, whereas `apply` will handle it in the background
`activityReceiver` \ is missing `RECEIVER_EXPORTED` or `RECEIVER_NOT_EXPORTED` flag for unprotected \ broadcasts registered for io.polyfence.ACTIVITY_UPDATE
Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `TrackingScheduler` which has field `context` pointing to `Context`); this is a memory leak
